text: Machine learning helps computers learn from data. One common type is supervised learning. Here, we give the computer a lot of examples with correct answers, called training data. For instance, we might show it many pictures labeled "cat" or "dog." The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules based on patterns it finds. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions about new, unseen data, such as identifying if a new photo shows a cat or a dog. Finally, we check its accuracy—the percentage of correct guesses—to see how well it has learned.
